metadata.use_case:
I need help designing a schema for analyzing public comments submitted to a federal regulatory docket regarding medical payment products (medical credit cards and loans). These comments were submitted in response to a joint Request for Information (RFI) from the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), and Department of the Treasury.

The comments come from diverse stakeholders:
- Individual patients sharing personal experiences with medical cre

policy_recommendations: Ban on Credit Reporting for Medical Debt

The CARE CREDIT medical credit card has kept me in debt. I mainly use it for my veterinary bills.
I pray that my service pet doesn’t get sick again because the interest rate is so high that I’m in debt immediately.
These products are nothing short of a rip-off. The prices are not negotiated (as they are if you are using insurance).
Our regulators should require that these products stay within the guidelines and, more importantly, that prices for the services match Medicare levels.
Them financing interest was greatly bloated. I feel the dental practitioner may have received a 'kickback' for pushing me in the direction of a particular loan agency.
Where the need for financing to overcome health issues is fair, it may be better to used a standard credit card.
Please see attached comments from the American Animal Hospital Association.
I informed them, I was on Medicare Disability... that third party charged Credit One since 2010 for disability premiums.
I filed a claim in 2019, I was told I did not qualify because I was disabled.
Manuel A. Cordero, DDS, CPH <ceo@hdassoc.org>

seeking to understand the effects that medical credit cards, loans, and other financial products have on patients

encourage CFPB, Treasury and HHS to further study the implications of use of such medical payment products... among patients receiving treatment for mental health conditions and substance use disorders.

This new practice of just signing patients up on admittance into finance contracts will put more people in financial jeopardy.
most bankruptcies are related to medical debt.
This predatory activity of pushing patients into finance contracts will only make matters worse for patients and their families.
Hospitals used to set up payment plans when needed, often at no interest, because they had the appearance of operating for the public good.
Most bankruptcies in the US are related to medical debt. This predatory activity... will only make matters worse.
If there were universal healthcare in the US we would not be having a discussion on financing health care debt at the point of service.

representative_narrative_snippets: Debt from life saving care should be treated differently than other debit. Stress caused by collection calls/unpaid bills is the least conducive thing to recovery.
I am shocked by how quickly medical debt is sent to collection agencies even by 'nonprofit' hospitals.
Response to Request for Information Regarding Medical Payment Products
unavailable
Medical debt is a glaring manifestation of the deeply flawed and corrupt health care system in our country.
It is an egregious injustice that leaves countless individuals drowning in an insurmountable burden, jeopardizing their financial stability.

unavailable
I actually find the Care Credit option of payment helpful.
I've found it to be helpful in stretching my budget and necessary for dental expenses or emergency bills.
My only relationship comes from emergency care under the Veterans Administration programs & they have been great in the last 2 years.
I haven't done the treatment I need yet because I can't pay for this over the course of a year and then I would have onerous credit terms.
the issue I find in my town is not being able to find a doctor or dentist who will accept Medicaid, especially for dental care.
We don't need these products, don't want these products, and will not involve ourselves by obtaining or utilizing these products.
Human Rights Watch strongly supports the agencies’ efforts to better regulate the rights-impacting practices of private entities.
Neighborhood Trust Financial Partners’ Comment in Response to Request for Information Regarding Medical Payment Products.
Care Credit offered a promo for 0% financiing for 24 months, but then an interest rate of 22.9% and above, with severe penalty APR if a payment was missed.
My health care provider only covered the removal of the cataract and a blank (non-RX) lens. I had to pay for the prescription lens.
I wish to record the issues I have with the Care Credit credit card issued by Synchrony Bank.
My full comment is too long to fit into the online form's 5,000 character limit, so I have attached it as a Word document.
The U.S. has some of the highest healthcare costs in the world, which can lead to significant financial burdens on patients.
Factors contributing to the medical debt crisis... include high healthcare costs and aggressive debt collection.
The medical billing practices that consumers (patients) must be a part of (be victims of) are for the most part unknown and mysterious.
Taking advantage of consumers (patients) when they are so vulnerable is IMMORAL.

If I didn't have regular credit cards, I would be walking around with a missing front tooth.

ACA International representing the accounts receivable management industry regarding medical debt collection and credit reporting.
I was treated at PeaceHealth St. Joseph Hospital... my bills were over $1 million.
The kicker, though, was aftercare. I will need chiropractic...
They bated me and painted the card like it was perfect for me.
My balance of $700 not significantly decreased despite making consistent and timely payments, but it has actually risen.
I don't always go to my doctor when it would probably be a good idea because my deductible is a few thousand dollars.
Now that my husband and I are newly retired and likely to need more medical care in the coming years...
Deferred payment plans provide a significant level of flexibility and financial relief to individuals facing unexpected expenses.
As a responsible credit card user, I believe that these options play a crucial role in providing financial flexibility.
We advocate for the development and implementation of data-driven public policy that improves child and family well-being.
Public policies and investments remove barriers for most impacted children and families.
As an immuno-compromised senior, the issue of financing health care debt is of great concern to me.
This predatory activity of pushing patients into finance contracts will only make matters worse for patients and their families and increase the number of bankruptcies.
My dentist has offered me a medical credit card to help finance a $5,000 medical dental repair that is not covered by my insurance.
My dentist acted offended that I would not use the credit card financing.
There are many different types of medical payment products. I'd be careful to lump them all into the same category.
Offering the financing to our patients is not our first option by any means, it is just there as another option for our patients in addition to applying for financial assistance.

policy_recommendations: Ban on Credit Reporting for Medical Debt

representative_narrative_snippets: via Electronic Submission Only: www.regulations.gov Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
I was a graduate student in college without any health care or dental care coverage when I had one of my molars collapse on me. I was in excruciating pain.
The dentist recommended that I open a Care Credit card for the $900 it would cost to have the work done. The only other option presented to me at the time was to continue on with my nerve exposed.
Please see the attached comments.
Please see the attached comment from the National Dental Association. Thank you.
I told the financial lady I would only pay the Medicare price for the visit. One month later I was billed $2,664 for an EKG and some labs.
They billed it as a level 4 ER visit which is upcoding.
No one should feel like they would rather risk their health, their life at times, just because they're unfamiliar with the healthcare system.
It is unconscionable that healthcare admins/non-profit hospitals... withhold VITAL information from their charges.

policy_recommendations: Ban on Credit Reporting for Medical Debt

representative_narrative_snippets: I've suddenly got a letter from a collections agency or it showed up on my credit report, that I suddenly owe a co-pay for a test or surgery.
I've always had insurance, and lately also Medicare & Medicaid- yet still, there have been several times where I suddenly got a letter from a collections agency.
Neither hospital, nor any clinic I've been to since has informed me of financial aid options.
If I miss a deadline once because of my Neurodiversity, I'm back on the chopping block with collections.
Hospitals should not be allowed to claim not for profit status while refusing to provide charity care programs to people in need.
My inability to pay for healthcare has prevented me from seeking out even basic appointments like physicals and check ups.
I believe they took advantage of me and my insurance company. They also did not offer any type of charity care which I could have qualified for.
This caused me to file bankruptcy.
Our policy has a $5,000 per year out-of-pocket maximum... this feels more like an out-of-pocket expectation or obligation in order to get health care.
Our gross income last year was $63,000, so $5,000 makes a difference to our household budget.
Subject: Ban Predatory Medical Payment Products -- Request for Information Regarding Medical Payment Products
The CFPB must strictly regulate or ban these products to protect patients from financial ruin.

policy_recommendations: Ban on Credit Reporting for Medical Debt

representative_narrative_snippets: Medical payment products just causes me more money with absolutely nothing to show for it.
Actual bills from hospitals actually easily cover the cost of a brand new car-something the vast majority of the people our congress represents cannot afford !!
I made a regrettable decision when I chose to utilize Care Credit... Care Credit materials seemed omnipresent in his office.
I realized that I was paying a staggering 26.99% interest rate... I've paid more in interest than the original cost of the dental procedure.
